Variable Description: Adaptive learning software uses algorithms or artificial intelligence to adapt content to individual learners. This may include tailoring the pace, difficulty, or format of instruction based on real-time assessments of student progress.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students advance to new topics when they have demonstrated mastery of previous competencies, rather than advancing with only a minimal passing grade.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Small groups of students meet regularly (daily or weekly, over multiple years) with adult advisors to set learning goals, reflect on progress, and build positive relationships.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students learn how artificial intelligence systems work, how to critically evaluate AI systems and their output, and how to use AI effectively and responsibly.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment uses artificial intelligence tools to help generate or adapt student-facing learning materials such as rubrics, assignments, assessments, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment uses artificial intelligence tools to help streamline teacher tasks such as lesson planning, project design, communication, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students get additional help and support through software powered by artificial intelligence. The program may give instant feedback, answer questions, and suggest practice problems tailored to each student’s needs, helping them learn at their own pace.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment is building a culture of taking direct action to dismantle policies and practices that create or perpetuate inequality between racial groups.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students engage in paid, multi-year training programs that combine on-the-job learning with related classroom instruction, often leading to industry credentials.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Tests are administered to measure student performance on various dimensions of self-directed learning (e.g., self-awareness, agency, goal-setting and planning, etc.)
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment assesses students' readiness to enter the workforce and pursue a career path.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment assesses deeper learning and 21st century skills beyond academic content mastery, e.g. communication, collaboration, critical thinking, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment assesses social-emotional and non-cognitive skills, e.g., self-management, social awareness, relationship skills, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students learn both online and in-person, in which both modalities are connected to provide an integrated learning experience, and with some element of student control over the time, place, path, or pace of learning.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students receive ongoing, individualized guidance to explore career interests, navigate pathways, reflect on work-based learning experiences, align coursework to short- and long-term goals, apply to programs or job opportunities, and prepare for next steps after graduation.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students regularly engage in structured career exploration (e.g., industry talks, job shadowing, job fairs, mock interviews, virtual career tours, panels, site visits) that broaden their understanding of future options.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description:
The career-connected learning question has the definition integrated as part of it but including for reference:
The school helps students link what they learn in school to real-world futures—through exploration, skill-building, guidance from trusted adults, and hands-on experiences.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: As part of their coursework, students work on real-world projects co-designed or sponsored by industry partners.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment involves community-based organizations in shared decision-making, and communicates how CBO input has informed decision-making. This may include strategies such as offering learning hubs and integrating expertise into schools.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment involves families in shared decision-making, and communicates how family input has informed decision-making. This may include strategies such as participatory budgeting, family advisory groups, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description:
The learning environment involves industry-based partners in shared decision-making, and communicates how these partners' input has informed decision-making. This may include strategies such as designing career pathways, developing curriculum and assessments, or offering validated work-based learning as a core part of the school's
design.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment involves students in shared decision-making, and communicates how student input has informed decision-making. This may include strategies such as student advisory councils, input on curriculum and culture-setting, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment involves teachers in shared decision-making, and communicates how teacher input has informed decision-making. Teachers make decisions in areas such as learning environment success, curriculum, budget, evaluating their colleagues, selecting personnel, and more.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment involves stakeholders in the process of decision-making and communicates how the input gathered from stakeholders informs decision-making. This may include teachers, students, families, community-based organizations, industry-based partners, or others.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students receive ongoing, individualized guidance to explore higher education options, align coursework to short- and long-term goals, apply to programs, complete the FAFSA application, and prepare for next steps after graduation.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Partnerships with businesses and the community help to facilitate experiential learning, internships, mentorships, tutoring, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment offers (or partners to offer) social and wraparound support services to meet the needs of families and the community at large, e.g. through medical care, nutritional services, adult learning, childcare, family counseling, financial advising.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment's model is designed to ensure students are becoming proficient by advancing on demonstrated mastery. May include a deliberate focus on student agency, transparent learning expectations, timely/differentiated supports, varied pacing and pathways, meaningful and formative assessment.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students alternate between school and structured, credit-bearing work placements connected to their studies.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Pedagogy and curriculum are designed to support students who have been marginalized to be independent learners. Core barriers to learning for historically marginalized learners are examined and removed.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment tracks data on the extent to which different groups of students (e.g., by race, gender, disability, socioeconomic status) participate in programs to ensure that students have equitable access.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: A form of bilingual education in which students are taught literacy and content in two languages.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: A hybrid of high school and college, where all students take both high school and college classes simultaneously, earning credit towards a college degree.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment increases total instructional time through a longer school day, extended school year, or year-round schedule.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment makes creative use of human capital, e.g. through a tutor residency model, outside experts, master teachers or teacher mentors, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Grades are reported on a standards-based report card, and focus on the specific competencies students are expected to develop.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Formal partnerships with colleges or training providers support students’ learning and postsecondary pathways. This may include dual credit or early college opportunities, transition advising, curriculum or enrichment partnerships, or school models embedded within or affiliated with a higher education institution.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students who typically receive pull-out services or instruction in segregated classrooms (e.g., students classified as English Learners, students with disabilities) are integrated with appropriate supports in place to support learning, language ability, and full participation in curriculum.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students can earn industry-issued or recognized credentials as a verification of a their qualifications or competence, in fields such as agriculture, hospitality & tourism, health, information technology, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Teachers or coaches with recent industry experience bring real-world expertise into instruction.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The school offers one or more sequences of learning (including both academic and technical courses) focused on a specific industry or career sector, designed to build depth of knowledge and skills related to that field.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students complete structured, supervised workplace experiences aligned to career interests, typically for a defined period and with reflection or credit tied to school goals.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Data from multiple platforms or software programs is integrated in a "data dashboard" reflecting overall student learning and progress.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Profiles extend across multiple years and may include how the student is performing, as well as personal factors like who they are, what inspires them, and how they best learn.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students follow unique learning pathways tailored to their needs, interests, and passions.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment offers (or partners to offer) comprehensive counseling and mental health services for students.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students connect with adults in and/or outside of the learning environment for 1:1 mentoring and regular check-in meetings.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students earn verified micro-credentials demonstrating skills linked to employability or technical competencies.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment has an explicit multi-tiered system of support wherein students progress is monitored for all students, and students who are progressing at a slower pace are provided with structured academic interventions aligned to their academic needs with varying tiers of intensity. Progress is tracked in an ongoing manner for all students to inform fluid intervention decisions.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Classrooms include students of many ages, rather than grouping them by age or grade band.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The school brokers connections between students and industry professionals, including methods such as job shadows, video chats, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Gifted programs are eliminated and barriers to advanced courses (e.g. AP, IB, honors) are removed so that no student is barred from accessing these opportunities. Remediation takes place throughout the regular scope and sequence so that "lower level" classes are removed.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students learn through answering complex questions or solving for real-world problems (e.g. homelessness in their city, or a marketing challenge faced by a real company). Also called problem-based learning, inquiry-based learning, or learning-by-doing.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment creates deliberate structures and processes for peer support, e.g. peer mentorship, peer tutoring, peer assessment, accountability partners, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students apply knowledge or demonstrate a skill in place of traditional assessment, for example, through portfolios, projects, and other displays of progress toward goals.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The school has adopted resources and materials designed based on what research shows facilitates effective teaching and learning experiences. High-quality materials have been rigorously reviewed (e.g., EdReports green rating).
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Distributing school resources equitably, so students receive support and resources according to their need rather than every student receiving the same supports. (Example: assigning most experienced and highest skilled teachers to students needing the most help.)
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Restorative practices are used to resolve conflict and strengthen relationships among individuals and within communities in ways that are intentionally restorative, developmentally appropriate, and equitably applied. (In contrast to reacting to harmful or hurtful behavior with punishment or suspension.)
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students operate a business or service as part of the learning model (e.g., student-run café, design studio).
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment uses a structured curriculum, program, or materials designed to help students develop social and emotional skills.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment integrates social and emotional learning throughout the academic curriculum and other activities, rather than as a stand-alone class.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students engage in meaningful community service to enrich the learning experience, learn civic responsibility, and strengthen their communities.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students plan and lead a meeting including parents and teachers to discuss the student's learning, growth, Individual Education Program (IEP) and/or reclassification from English Learner services if applicable. (As opposed to parent-teacher conferences without students present.)
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students (and families) can access their own learning and assessment data in real time.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students set and pursue their own learning goals, as well as self-assess their progress.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students determine their own pace in learning rather than being required to move at the same pace as a whole group.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students are partners and leaders in designing, implementing, and assessing projects.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Adults are trained to recognize and respond to those who have been impacted by traumatic stress, and students are provided with clear expectations and communication strategies to guide them through stressful situations.
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students regularly experience 1:1 or small-group teaching toward a specific learning goal. Note: In high-impact tutoring, students work with a consistent tutor, multiple times per week, in sessions that are integrated with the learning environment's curriculum and schedule (as opposed to a supplemental or out-of-school program).
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: The learning environment is designed to proactively meet the needs of all learners by identifying barriers to learning in the design of the environment, not in the student. This helps educators differentiate to effectively instruct a diverse group of students, regardless of whether students have an Individualized Education Program (IEP).
Corresponding Survey Question: Are any of the following practices being implemented school- or site-wide this school year?

Variable Description: Students learn how artificial intelligence systems work, how to critically evaluate AI systems and their output, and how to use AI effectively and responsibly.

Variable Description: A form of bilingual education in which students are taught literacy and content in two languages.

Variable Description: The learning environment makes creative use of human capital, e.g. through a tutor residency model, outside experts, master teachers or teacher mentors, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Grades are reported on a standards-based report card, and focus on the specific competencies students are expected to develop.

Variable Description: Students who typically receive pull-out services or instruction in segregated classrooms (e.g., students classified as English Learners, students with disabilities) are integrated with appropriate supports in place to support learning, language ability, and full participation in curriculum.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Students can earn industry-issued or recognized credentials as a verification of a their qualifications or competence, in fields such as agriculture, hospitality & tourism, health, information technology, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Teachers or coaches with recent industry experience bring real-world expertise into instruction.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Students complete structured, supervised workplace experiences aligned to career interests, typically for a defined period and with reflection or credit tied to school goals.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Data from multiple platforms or software programs is integrated in a "data dashboard" reflecting overall student learning and progress.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Profiles extend across multiple years and may include how the student is performing, as well as personal factors like who they are, what inspires them, and how they best learn.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Students follow unique learning pathways tailored to their needs, interests, and passions.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: The learning environment offers (or partners to offer) comprehensive counseling and mental health services for students.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Students connect with adults in and/or outside of the learning environment for 1:1 mentoring and regular check-in meetings.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Students earn verified micro-credentials demonstrating skills linked to employability or technical competencies.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: The learning environment has an explicit multi-tiered system of support wherein students progress is monitored for all students, and students who are progressing at a slower pace are provided with structured academic interventions aligned to their academic needs with varying tiers of intensity. Progress is tracked in an ongoing manner for all students to inform fluid intervention decisions.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Classrooms include students of many ages, rather than grouping them by age or grade band.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: The school brokers connections between students and industry professionals, including methods such as job shadows, video chats, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Gifted programs are eliminated and barriers to advanced courses (e.g. AP, IB, honors) are removed so that no student is barred from accessing these opportunities. Remediation takes place throughout the regular scope and sequence so that "lower level" classes are removed.

Variable Description: Students learn through answering complex questions or solving for real-world problems (e.g. homelessness in their city, or a marketing challenge faced by a real company). Also called problem-based learning, inquiry-based learning, or learning-by-doing.

Variable Description: The learning environment creates deliberate structures and processes for peer support, e.g. peer mentorship, peer tutoring, peer assessment, accountability partners, etc.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: Students apply knowledge or demonstrate a skill in place of traditional assessment, for example, through portfolios, projects, and other displays of progress toward goals.
Corresponding Survey Question: Of all the practices you just selected, which five do you believe to be most central to your learning environment’s design?

Variable Description: The school has adopted resources and materials designed based on what research shows facilitates effective teaching and learning experiences. High-quality materials have been rigorously reviewed (e.g., EdReports green rating).

Variable Description: Distributing school resources equitably, so students receive support and resources according to their need rather than every student receiving the same supports. (Example: assigning most experienced and highest skilled teachers to students needing the most help.)

Variable Description: Restorative practices are used to resolve conflict and strengthen relationships among individuals and within communities in ways that are intentionally restorative, developmentally appropriate, and equitably applied. Variable Description: Students engage in meaningful community service to enrich the learning experience, learn civic responsibility, and strengthen their communities.

Variable Description: Students are partners and leaders in designing, implementing, and assessing projects.

Variable Description: Adults are trained to recognize and respond to those who have been impacted by traumatic stress, and students are provided with clear expectations and communication strategies to guide them through stressful situations.
